title: Customers With Consistent Purchase Gap
description: Identify customers whose gap between consecutive orders is always less than or equal to 30 days.
sql_solution: SELECT customer_id FROM (SELECT customer_id, julianday(date) - julianday(LAG(date) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY date)) gap FROM orders) GROUP BY customer_id HAVING MAX(COALESCE(gap,0)) <= 30;
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

gap_window = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(to_date(col("date")))

customer_gaps = (
    orders.withColumn("prev_date", lag("date").over(gap_window))
    .withColumn("gap", datediff(to_date(col("date")), to_date(col("prev_date"))))
)

result = (
    customer_gaps.groupBy("customer_id")
    .agg(max(coalesce(col("gap"), lit(0))).alias("max_gap"))
    .filter(col("max_gap") <= 30)
    .select("customer_id")
)
